history.qwen_observation: The build failed on JDK 8 because the Maven compiler plugin was configured to use the --release flag, which is unsupported in JDK 8.
history.qwen_rationale: Setting the java.version property to 8 in the first step ensures the compiler plugin targets JDK 8, preventing the --release flag error and allowing the initial Lombok bump to compile successfully before proceeding with the Java version upgrades.

history.qwen_rationale: Changing the execution JDK for the initial step to 11 allows the compiler to correctly process --release 8 (since JDK 9+ supports the --release flag), resolving the immediate compilation error while preserving the migration path. I also pruned unsupported recipes from the allowed primitives list to prevent resolution failures.

history.qwen_rationale: Adding explicit javax.xml.bind:jaxb-api and org.glassfish.jaxb:jaxb-runtime dependencies to the first step will provide the missing JAXB classes required for compilation on JDK 11+, allowing the Lombok bump and initial build check to succeed.

Subsequent steps will incrementally bump Java versions and migrate to Spring Boot 3.2/Jakarta for Java 21 compatibility.

history.qwen_observation: The build failed on JDK 11 because the maven-compiler-plugin remained at 3.8.0 (likely managed by parent) and JAXB classes were missing from the classpath, causing NoClassDefFoundError during compilation.
history.qwen_rationale: Splitting the initial step isolates the compiler/JAXB fix from the heavy Spring Boot upgrade. Explicitly adding the maven-compiler-plugin.version property forces the plugin upgrade, while adding JAXB dependencies resolves the JDK 11+ missing classes. Upgrading Spring Boot to 2.7 in a separate step ensures a stable baseline before moving to Java 17 and Spring Boot 3.x.
